note: Mathlib/Topology/MetricSpace/GromovHausdorff.lean:86 defines GHSpace = nonempty compact metric spaces up to isometry with `instance : MetricSpace GHSpace where` at line 394 (dist = ghDist, defined line 176) — the Gromov-Hausdorff distance itself, so convergence in this metric (generic Tendsto/nhds machinery) literally is Gromov-Hausdorff convergence; restricted to the compact case (no pointed/non-compact GH convergence in Mathlib).
verify_note: GHSpace (Mathlib/Topology/MetricSpace/GromovHausdorff.lean:86, MetricSpace instance:394) is the TYPE of nonempty compact metric spaces up to isometry plus its GH-distance instance -- the ambient space -- not a convergence predicate; grep across every GromovHausdorff*.lean file finds zero named GH-convergence decl (only internal proof-step Tendsto calls), and the repo's own house convention for 'X convergence' concepts (e.g. Uniform convergence -> TendstoUniformly) expects a decl that IS the convergence relation, not the space it would be defined over; 'exact' overclaims a derived-generic notion as a formalization.

note: Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/CrossProduct.lean:110-113, docstring literally: 'The scalar quadruple product identity, related to the Binet-Cauchy identity.' States (u×v)·(w×x) = (u·w)(v·x) − (u·x)(v·w) for R^3 via the cross product — the n=3 special case of the general n-dimensional Binet-Cauchy identity (sum over 2-subsets of minors), not the general identity itself.
verify_note: Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/CrossProduct.lean:110-113 cross_dot_cross docstring literally reads 'related to the Binet-Cauchy identity'; formula u×v . w×x = (u.w)(v.x) - (u.x)(v.w) is exactly the n=3 instance of the general Binet-Cauchy sum-over-2-minors identity (Wikidata Q935404: 'generalizes Lagrange's identity, determining the expansion of the product of two series') -- special_case is the honest label, not exact, and the row correctly uses special_case.

note: Mathlib/Topology/Compactness/Lindelof.lean:482: `class LindelofSpace (X : Type*) [TopologicalSpace X] : Prop where` (module docstring: 'LindelofSpace X: X is Lindelöf if it is Lindelöf as a set') — the direct formalization of a Lindelöf topological space, with NonLindelofSpace, HereditarilyLindelofSpace and the full IsLindelof API alongside it.
verify_note: Mathlib/Topology/Compactness/Lindelof.lean:482 `class LindelofSpace (X : Type*) [TopologicalSpace X] : Prop where isLindelof_univ : IsLindelof univ`, and isLindelof_univ_iff/isLindelof_iff_countable_subcover give 'every open cover has a countable subcover' -- verbatim Wikidata Q936155 description; line number, class name, and semantics all check out.
